title: Conductive flexible and stretchable encapsulation method and apparatus
abstract: Apparatus and methods are provided for flexible, stretchable, wearable electronics. In an example, an apparatus for providing flexible and stretchable conductors can include a first elastomer layer, conductive ink applied to the first elastomer layer, and an adhesive layer, in cooperation with the first elastomer layer, configured to encapsulate the conductive ink, the adhesive layer further configured to allow the apparatus to be attached to a second apparatus.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. An apparatus for providing flexible and stretchable conductors; the apparatus comprising: a first elastomer layer; conductive ink applied to the first elastomer layer; and an adhesive layer, in cooperation with the first elastomer layer, configured to encapsulate the conductive ink, the adhesive layer configured to allow the apparatus to be attached to a second apparatus. 2. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the first elastomer layer includes a thermoplastic elastomer. 3. The apparatus of claim 2, wherein the thermoplastic elastomer includes thermoplastic polyurethane. 4. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the adhesive layer includes a thermoplastic elastomer. 5. The apparatus of claim 4, wherein the thermoplastic elastomer includes thermoplastic polyurethane. 6. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the adhesive layer is configured to allow the apparatus to be attached to a garment. 7. The apparatus of claim 6, wherein the adhesive layer is configured to seal a seam of the garment. 8. The apparatus of claim 6, wherein the adhesive layer is configured to waterproof a seam of the garment. 9. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the adhesive layer is configured to allow the apparatus to be attached to a user's skin. 10. A method comprising: printing circuit traces on a elastomer substrate using conductive ink; encapsulating the circuit traces using an adhesive layer coupled to the elastomeric substrate. 11. The method of claim 10, wherein the printing includes printing the circuit traces on a thermoplastic elastomer substrate using the conductive ink. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein the printing includes printing the circuit traces on a thermoplastic polyurethane substrate using the conductive ink. 13. The method of claim 10, wherein the encapsulating includes encapsulating the circuit traces using seam tape coupled to the elastomeric substrate. 14. The method of claim 10, wherein the adhesive layer includes a thermoplastic elastomer. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the thermoplastic elastomer includes thermoplastic polyurethane. 16. The method of claim 10, wherein the encapsulation includes hot pressing the elastomer substrate with the adhesive layer. 17. The method of claim 10, wherein the printing includes curing the conductive ink. 18. The method of claim 10, including attaching the adhesive layer to a garment. 19. The method of claim 18, wherein the attaching includes hot pressing the adhesive layer to the garment. 20. The method of claim 10, including attaching the adhesive layer to the body of a user.
